summary: CC-0109 (Model B) adds an opt-in, feature-gated CronJob that mints a fresh Keystone admin password, validates and commits it onto an operator-owned push-source Secret, and mirrors it to OpenBao via a PushSecret at the shared bootstrap/keystone-admin path so the Part-1 (CC-0108) ESO->Secret->re-bootstrap path cuts the credential over. The implementation faithfully reuses the existing split-compute-write primitives (staging Secret, split RBAC, observeRotationAge, EnsurePushSecret, CreateImmutableConfigMap) without modifying applyRotationOutput/validateRotationOutput. CRD is regenerated byte-identically across both representations, PasswordRotationReady is wired into subConditionTypes + subReconcilerConditionTypes, the OpenBao write grant is narrowly scoped to the two exact data+metadata leaves and bound only on the management role while eso-management stays read-only, and the clobber-safe gate + apply validator prevent any empty/short password reaching OpenBao. Build, vet, unit, webhook, metrics, and envtest suites all pass; docs are accurate with no drift. All BLOCKER and CRITICAL acceptance criteria are met. Verdict is NEEDS_CHANGES for a set of consistency/robustness gaps that match documented external-review blindspots: a missing webhook defense-in-depth check on passwordLength (every other Minimum-marked field has one), the aggregate validation sentinel test not being extended to the new passwordRotation rules, an inaccurate OpenBao policy comment that justifies the delete capability via a DeletionPolicy the code does not use, an E2E step-4 script timeout (300s) far below its worst-case loop duration (~1020s), and the E2E catch block omitting operator/OpenBao logs.

tests_checklist.item: Tests exist and pass (unit, webhook, metrics, envtest all green; e2e suite present)
Edge cases covered (missing/empty/short password, missing/malformed annotation, absent staging, clobber-safe gate, idempotent teardown)
Aggregate validation sentinel test (TestValidateCreate_RunsAllValidations) updated for the new passwordRotation validation paths
E2E step-4 script timeout exceeds worst-case cumulative poll duration
RBAC split-shape asserted robustly (find-by-resource, not index-coupled)

code_quality_checklist.item: Follows existing split-compute-write rotation pattern (reconcile_fernet.go) and optional-pointer sub-reconciler convention
applyRotationOutput / validateRotationOutput unchanged; Model B uses additive password-shaped variants
Defense-in-depth webhook checks applied consistently across all +kubebuilder:validation:Minimum-marked fields
Shell test file naming consistent with the *_test.sh convention used by sibling tests/unit/deploy files

security_checklist.item: OpenBao write grant scoped to exactly kv-v2/data+metadata/bootstrap/keystone-admin (no wildcard/+ glob), bound only on management role; eso-management stays read-only (CC-0083 invariant preserved)
PushSecret to bootstrap/keystone-admin never created/active with an empty or invalid password (clobber-safe gate adminPasswordPushSourceReady + apply validator, fail-closed)
CronJob ServiceAccount limited to get+patch on the staging Secret only (no production/push-source write, no create/delete)
Generated password stdlib-only (secrets.token_urlsafe) and always >= operator minimum length; no secret in process args or logs
Security-sensitive policy comments accurately describe the actual grant/lifecycle

architecture_checklist.item: Reuses existing primitives; minimal marginal surface (one script, one sub-reconciler, one CRD field, one policy)
push-source Secret (<cr>-admin-password-next) is a distinct object from the ESO creationPolicy:Owner admin Secret
Single-CR / shared-path assumption (boundary 8 option a) is an explicit, documented decision
E2E isolation avoids leaving shared production OpenBao state (bootstrap/keystone-admin) rotated without restoration

dry_yagni_checklist.item: No duplication of the fernet/credential apply/validate logic (additive variants only)
Reuses ensureStagingSecret, observeRotationAge, secrets.EnsurePushSecret, config.CreateImmutableConfigMap, deleteCronJob, PruneImmutableConfigMaps

fail_fast_checklist.item: Apply validator + clobber-safe gate fail-closed on empty/short password or read error
Webhook rejects malformed/empty cron and missing adminPasswordSecretRef when rotation is enabled
Webhook enforces the passwordLength minimum as defense-in-depth (parity with fernet/credential maxActiveKeys)

defensive_checklist.item: Inputs validated at admission (webhook) and apply (validateAdminPasswordRotationOutput) boundaries
Teardown tolerates NotFound and is idempotent (verified by unit + envtest)
PushSecret DeletionPolicy=None prevents admin lockout when rotation is disabled

security_findings.description: push-keystone-admin.hcl justifies the high-trust `delete` capability on the admin bootstrap data+metadata paths via 'the openbao-finalizer drives PushSecret deletion with DeletionPolicy=Delete (CC-0079)', but adminPasswordPushSecret sets DeletionPolicy=None. ESO therefore never issues a remote delete for this PushSecret, so the `delete` grant is never exercised by Model B and the comment misrepresents the lifecycle on the most security-sensitive grant in the change (boundary 5). This is a least-privilege + comment-accuracy concern on the admin credential path (check-comments-describe-the-actual-invariant).
security_findings.fix: Correct the comment to state DeletionPolicy=None and that `delete` is retained only for teardown/portability parity with push-keystone-keys.hcl (not exercised by the openbao-finalizer/DeletionPolicy=Delete path, which applies to fernet/credential rotation); or, for tighter least-privilege on the admin path, drop `delete` since it is never used while DeletionPolicy=None.

architecture_findings.description: The E2E suite writes and rotates the SHARED production OpenBao object bootstrap/keystone-admin (forced by the hardcoded PushSecret RemoteKey — boundary 8 option a) and re-seeds it at the start, but never restores it on completion. It relies solely on concurrent:false + ephemeral kind clusters to bound the blast radius. On a persistent/shared cluster, any suite running after this one (and the production keystone-admin ExternalSecret) would observe the rotated value, which can re-bootstrap unrelated Keystone CRs. The decision is documented, but leaving shared state rotated is an isolation smell relative to CC-0093/CC-0108's parallel-safe dedicated paths.
architecture_findings.fix: Add a cleanup/finally step that re-seeds bootstrap/keystone-admin back to the known initial password after the suite (or document explicitly that the suite is kind-ephemeral-only and must never run on a shared persistent cluster).

issues_found.description: BLOCKER (per agent) / MAJOR reliability: the Step-4 rotation-verification script declares timeout: 300s, but its 8 sequential polling loops plus the 120s `kubectl wait` sum to a worst-case ~1020s. On a slow CI runner Chainsaw kills the script mid-execution at 300s, masking the real failure with a generic script-timeout (test-script-timeout-must-exceed-worst-case-loop-duration, CC-0076).
issues_found.fix: Raise the script timeout to comfortably exceed the worst case (e.g. timeout: 20m), and/or tighten the per-loop iteration bounds so the cumulative worst case stays under the configured timeout with margin.

issues_found.description: Webhook defense-in-depth inconsistency: PasswordRotationSpec.PasswordLength carries +kubebuilder:validation:Minimum=24 but validate() has no corresponding runtime guard, whereas every other Minimum-marked field in this webhook (Fernet/CredentialKeys MaxActiveKeys>=3, UWSGI Harakiri/HTTPKeepAliveTimeout>=1, TerminationGracePeriodSeconds>=10) DOES have a paired defense-in-depth check. If CRD schema admission is bypassed, an out-of-range passwordLength is not rejected at admission (apply-defense-in-depth-checks-consistently). The runtime is saved only by the reconciler floor in normalizedAdminPasswordLength, but the established webhook convention is not followed.
issues_found.fix: In the `pr != nil && pr.Enabled` block, add a guard rejecting pr.PasswordLength != 0 && pr.PasswordLength < 24 with a field.Invalid on spec.bootstrap.passwordRotation.passwordLength, mirroring the Fernet.MaxActiveKeys `< 3 && != 0` pattern; add a webhook unit test for it.
issues_found.evidence: validate() passwordRotation block (lines 406-426) checks Schedule and AdminPasswordSecretRef.Name only; there is no `pr.PasswordLength < 24` check. Compare keystone_webhook.go:264-270 (Fernet maxActiveKeys defense-in-depth).

issues_found.description: The aggregate sentinel test TestValidateCreate_RunsAllValidations — whose stated purpose is to exercise every validation rule simultaneously and prove no short-circuiting — was not extended to cover the new passwordRotation validation paths (enabled + bad/empty schedule, missing adminPasswordSecretRef). A future change that short-circuits before reaching the passwordRotation block would go undetected (update-aggregate-validation-tests-when-adding-new-validation-paths, CC-0075/CC-0084 precedent).
issues_found.fix: Add a broken passwordRotation case to the aggregate setup (e.g. PasswordRotation{Enabled:true, Schedule:"bad-cron"} and clear AdminPasswordSecretRef.Name) and assert ContainSubstring("passwordRotation") / "adminPasswordSecretRef" alongside the existing substring assertions.
issues_found.evidence: The setup (lines 1388-1496) and assertions (lines 1501-1519) never touch k.Spec.Bootstrap.PasswordRotation; no `passwordRotation` substring is asserted.

issues_found.description: Brittle index-based RBAC PolicyRule assertions couple the split-RBAC test to the builder's rule ordering. The security-critical exact-verb match is present (good), but role.Rules[0]/[1] indexing will silently mis-assert or break confusingly if a rule is added or reordered (avoid-brittle-index-based-container-assertions).
issues_found.fix: Locate each rule by its ResourceNames (push-source vs staging) before asserting Verbs, e.g. a findPolicyRuleByResourceName helper or ContainElements with field matchers, so the assertion is order-independent.
issues_found.evidence: g.Expect(role.Rules[0].Verbs).To(Equal([]string{"get"})) ... g.Expect(role.Rules[1].Verbs).To(Equal([]string{"get", "patch"})) — both rely on a fixed slice index.

issues_found.description: Step-4 catch block omits the two most authoritative diagnostic sources for a rotation/push failure: the operator controller logs (current and --previous) and the OpenBao logs. A PushSecret 403 against bootstrap/keystone-admin is most authoritatively diagnosed from OpenBao's logs, and a stuck apply/commit from the operator log (ensure-test-error-capture-blocks-collect-sufficient-diagnostic-context, CC-0083).
issues_found.fix: Append operator-namespace logs (--tail with and without --previous) and OpenBao pod logs to the Step-4 catch script, matching the diagnostics depth of other rotation suites.
issues_found.evidence: Catch captures rotation/bootstrap Job pods, Keystone API pods, Keystone conditions, ExternalSecret + PushSecret status, secret metadata, events (lines 407-443) but no keystone-operator-system controller logs and no openbao-system logs.

issues_found.description: tests/unit/deploy/test_push_keystone_admin_policy.sh uses the test_*.sh PREFIX naming while all four existing tests/unit/deploy files use the *_test.sh SUFFIX convention, which forced an extra (broad) Makefile glob `tests/unit/deploy/test_*.sh`. Inconsistent and a precedent for over-broad globs.
issues_found.fix: Rename to push_keystone_admin_policy_test.sh and revert the Makefile glob addition so the single existing `*_test.sh` glob captures it.
issues_found.evidence: Existing: chaos_mesh_overlay_test.sh, flux_system_fluxinstance_test.sh, kind_base_flux_web_test.sh, production_posture_test.sh (suffix); new file uses prefix; Makefile diff adds `tests/unit/deploy/test_*.sh`.

issues_found.description: The full enabled-path reconcile is never exercised with Suspend=true; TestAdminPasswordRotationCronJob_Suspend covers only the isolated builder. (Envtest covers the suspend-without-deleting-siblings case, so this is a small unit-level gap.)
issues_found.fix: Add a reconcile-level variant that sets Suspend=true and asserts the created CronJob has spec.suspend=true with siblings intact.
issues_found.evidence: TestReconcilePasswordRotation_Enabled_HappyPath uses pwRotationTestKeystone() (Suspend:false at line 73); no enabled-path reconcile sets Suspend=true.

issues_found.description: Feature ID CC-0109 appears only once across the 636-line reconcile_passwordrotation_test.go, where the project convention (and the MINOR review criterion) is for new tests to cite the feature ID. The production file references it consistently; the test file does not.
issues_found.fix: Add (CC-0109, REQ-xxx) references to the per-function doc comments to keep traceability consistent with the rest of the package.

issues_found.description: teardownPasswordRotation issues ~6 Deletes plus a ConfigMap prune-list on every reconcile of every Keystone CR while rotation is disabled (the default). This follows the established optional-sub-reconciler teardown pattern but is heavier (multiple objects + a List) than the single-object teardowns elsewhere, adding steady-state API churn for the common case where the feature was never enabled.
issues_found.fix: Optional: short-circuit the teardown when no Model B objects exist (e.g. a cheap existence probe on the CronJob) before issuing the delete fan-out; or accept as-is for parity with sibling sub-reconcilers. Document the choice if kept.
issues_found.evidence: Disabled/nil path calls teardownPasswordRotation unconditionally (lines 166-178); teardown deletes CronJob + 2 Secrets + RoleBinding + Role + SA + PushSecret and prunes ConfigMaps every pass.

suggested_improvements: Add the passwordLength defense-in-depth guard and a paired webhook test, then extend TestValidateCreate_RunsAllValidations with the passwordRotation case so the regression guard stays complete.
Raise the Step-4 chainsaw script timeout (and/or tighten loop bounds) and enrich the Step-4 catch block with operator (--previous) and OpenBao logs.
Fix the push-keystone-admin.hcl comment to match DeletionPolicy=None (and reconsider whether the `delete` capability is needed on the admin path).
Make the split-RBAC test assertions order-independent (find rule by ResourceNames) and assert APIGroups=[""]/Resources=["secrets"] for completeness.
Rename the shell test to push_keystone_admin_policy_test.sh and revert the broadened Makefile glob.
Consider restoring bootstrap/keystone-admin at the end of the E2E suite (or documenting the kind-ephemeral-only constraint) to bound cross-suite blast radius.

Bump the architecture/ submodule pointer once the C5C3/C5C3 Model-B doc PR (task 7.3) merges — currently deferred and not present in this branch (correctly, since submodule files must not be edited from the worktree).
next_steps: Add webhook defense-in-depth check for spec.bootstrap.passwordRotation.passwordLength (< 24 when non-zero) + unit test.
Extend TestValidateCreate_RunsAllValidations to include a broken passwordRotation case and assert it accumulates.
Raise tests/e2e/.../chainsaw-test.yaml Step-4 script timeout above the ~1020s worst case; add operator + OpenBao logs to its catch block.
Correct the delete-capability rationale comment in push-keystone-admin.hcl (DeletionPolicy=None).
Refactor the split-RBAC unit assertions to be order-independent; optionally assert APIGroups/Resources.
Rename test_push_keystone_admin_policy.sh to the *_test.sh convention and revert the Makefile glob; add CC-0109 references throughout the test file.
